description: A Japanese noodle soup consisting of wheat noodles served in a seasoned broth, commonly topped with items such as sliced pork, egg, scallions, seaweed, and bamboo shoots. Different regions and shops are known for distinct broth styles.
core_ingredients: Seasoning base such as shoyu, miso, shio, or tonkotsu
preparation_steps: Prepare the broth and seasoning base.
Cook the noodles separately until just tender.
Place the noodles in a bowl and add the hot broth.

nutrition.fat: Varies from low to high depending on broth and toppings

health_advice.benefits: Can provide a comforting mix of carbohydrates, fluid, and protein.
health_advice.risks: Sodium is often high, and rich broths can also be high in saturated fat.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Choose more vegetables and leaner toppings, and avoid drinking all of a very salty broth if sodium intake is a concern.

dietary_notes.note: Vegetarian and vegan versions are possible with vegetable broth and suitable toppings. Traditional ramen noodles are made from wheat and are not gluten-free.

allergens: Soy in many broths and seasonings
